inputs.label: First Unit Time (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Standard time to produce the first unit, typically from historical data or pilot run.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard time to produce the first unit, typically from historical data or pilot run.

inputs.label: Learning Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage reduction in time per doubling of cumulative units. Typical range 70-95% for manual tasks.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage reduction in time per doubling of cumulative units. Typical range 70-95% for manual tasks.

inputs.label: Cumulative Units Produced
inputs.businessContext: Total number of units produced to date, used to estimate current and future unit times.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total number of units produced to date, used to estimate current and future unit times.

inputs.label: Target Units for Estimate
inputs.businessContext: The specific unit number for which the time estimate is required.

businessContext_i18n.en: The specific unit number for which the time estimate is required.

inputs.label: Shift Length (hours)
inputs.businessContext: Standard working shift duration for converting time to calendar days.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard working shift duration for converting time to calendar days.

inputs.label: Learning Curve Model
inputs.businessContext: Select the theoretical model: Wright (standard), Crawford (unit), or DeJong (plateau).

businessContext_i18n.en: Select the theoretical model: Wright (standard), Crawford (unit), or DeJong (plateau).

inputs.label: Include Break-In Effect
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies a penalty factor for production interruptions (e.g., after holidays or retooling).

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, applies a penalty factor for production interruptions (e.g., after holidays or retooling).

inputs.label: Break-In Penalty (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage increase in time for the first unit after a break, applied only if break-in is enabled.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage increase in time for the first unit after a break, applied only if break-in is enabled.

rules.message: Learning rate must be between 50% and 100%.
Target units must be greater than or equal to cumulative units for forward estimation.
Break-in penalty must be a positive percentage when break-in effect is enabled.

thresholds.alert: Learning rate below 70% indicates very steep curve; verify data source.
First unit time exceeds 500 hours; consider process redesign or automation.
Target units exceed 10,000; ensure learning model is validated for high volumes.

formulas.label: Learning Exponent (b)
formulas.description: Calculates the exponent from the learning rate using Wright's model.

formulas.label: Time for Target Unit (T_n)
formulas.description: Estimates the time to produce the target unit using the selected model.

formulas.label: Cumulative Average Time (C_n)
formulas.description: Average time per unit for all units up to target unit.
formulas.formula: C_n = first_unit_time * (target_units ^ b) / (1 + b)   [Wright cumulative average]

formulas.label: Total Time for Target Units (T_total)
formulas.description: Total cumulative time to produce all units up to target.

formulas.label: Break-In Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Applies penalty if break-in effect is enabled.
formulas.formula: if include_break_in then factor = 1 + (break_in_penalty/100) else factor = 1

formulas.label: Adjusted Time for Target Unit
formulas.description: Time for target unit after applying break-in adjustment.

formulas.label: Calendar Days to Reach Target
formulas.description: Converts total time to calendar days assuming one shift per day.

primary.label: Estimated Time for Target Unit
primary.description: The primary result: predicted time to produce the target unit, adjusted for break-in if applicable.

time_for_target_unit_raw.label: Raw Time for Target Unit (no adjustment)

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Learning Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Learning rate below 75% may indicate high variability or poor training.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High First Unit Time
hiddenLossDrivers.description: First unit time > 300 hours suggests process inefficiency.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Break-In Penalty Applied
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Adds up to 50% to first post-break unit time.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Operator Training
suggestedActions.description: Standardize training to achieve learning rate > 85%.
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces target unit time by up to 20%.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce First Unit Time
suggestedActions.description: Use pre-production planning and simulation to lower first unit time.

suggestedActions.label: Minimize Production Breaks
suggestedActions.description: Implement continuous flow to avoid break-in penalties.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Time
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Time estimate adjusted by a confidence factor based on input variability (e.g., ±10% for typical industrial data).
